author: writer-4
authorized_by: coordinator directive 2026-07-12 (register the rules-1-c mechanical verdict; RULES-2's frozen sequencing gate — modal_rules2._launch_gates, PROPOSED-ASM-1420/1807/1847 — requires registry/verdicts/rules-1-c.json to EXIST); scope-note discipline docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md 'Scope note'; precedents registry/corrections/f1/1-prefreeze-correction.json (pre-signoff reset-refreeze) and registry/corrections/a5-llm/1-posthoc-pin-erratum.json (which rules the reset path UNLAWFUL exactly when final-phase runs exist — here they did not).
changes: pins.analysis_script SWAPPED to the stdin-conformant successor: path analysis/rules_1c.py (sha256 bf4e6476aa72a060100df4681341d78f3f2ea598e7e640a1131153e4f004627c) -> analysis/rules_1c_stdin.py (sha256 b4fc99000140e29d678dd1a5aa14b65777809a51354f4404ed04019b7436748c). pins.analysis_script.output_fields UNCHANGED (37 declared pointers, byte-identical), so every frozen verdict-rule/endpoint metric pointer resolves exactly as before.

NO OTHER FIELD CHANGED: a field-by-field flatten-diff of the superseded frozen record vs the refrozen record shows exactly four deltas — /pins/analysis_script/path, /pins/analysis_script/sha256, /frozen_at (2026-07-12T14:07:42Z -> 2026-07-12T19:36:19Z), /frozen_sha256. Endpoints, verdict_rules, design (arms/IVs/seeds/floors), kill text, envelope, corpus pins, artifact_hashes (incl. certificate-result e0071e9e), harness_manifest placeholder, hypotheses, budget: byte-identical.
defect: INTERFACE DEFECT (mechanical, not scientific): the superseded record pinned analysis/rules_1c.py, whose input contract is argparse-REQUIRED flags (--run-records/--certificate), while tools/registry/verdict-gen.py step 5 (P2 section 3.1) invokes the pinned analysis with the eligible results-log run records as JSONL on STDIN and no argv. The pinned script would exit 2 (argparse) on every automated invocation => ERR_P2_ANALYSIS: the frozen record could never produce ANY verdict through the registered pure-function grader, including the INSTRUMENT-INVALID readout its own rule 0 mandates on the landed campaign (engagement_valid=false). The defect was latent at freeze because rules-1/rules-1-b inherited the same flag-based analysis shape and no rules-1 lineage record had ever reached verdict-gen.
kill_criterion_status: kill_criterion_verbatim UNCHANGED byte-identical (KILL-a executed pre-freeze/not fired; KILL-b text identical); extrapolation_envelope_verbatim UNCHANGED byte-identical.
legitimacy: Lawful pre-registration correction, NOT a post-hoc amendment (scope note: reset-correct-refreeze is lawful ONLY for a record NOT GNG-0-signed AND with NO final-phase run in its results-log; conditions conjunctive): at correction time rules-1-c was absent from registry/gng0-signoff.json frozen_records AND results-log/rules-1-c.jsonl did not exist (no run record of any phase, no unblind) — the P-9 cutoff was not crossed. The 13,470-row merged campaign bytes (poc/rules-1/results-incoming/20260712-142704-rules1b-parallel/merged/run-records-rules1b.jsonl, sha256 c1f5bea8241bed557e74535af3726c8384462bc69e8e8952918725f976f463be) sat in results-incoming, NEVER appended, so no logged evidence predates the refreeze. The swap is outcome-neutral by construction: the successor's statistics are byte-carried and the verdict on these rows is INSTRUMENT-INVALID under BOTH scripts (rule 0, engagement gate) — the correction cannot move the verdict, only make it producible. Reset to DRAFT, corrected, re-frozen through prereg-freeze under the full freeze-time lints (P-6 pin re-verification, corpus-pin recompute, pointer/output_fields check; same non-fatal PAUSE flag ASM-1134 re-emitted).
parity_proof: analysis/rules_1c_stdin.py byte-carries every statistic, constant, gate expression and the output serialization from analysis/rules_1c.py; only input acquisition changed (stdin eligible-records + pinned row/certificate paths with sha256 re-verification, fail closed ERR_RULES1C_*). PROOF: (a) python3 analysis/rules_1c.py --run-records <merged 13,470 rows> --certificate poc/rules-1/results/certificate-result.json and (b) a verdict-gen-shaped stdin invocation of analysis/rules_1c_stdin.py over 15 stamped eligible run records covering the same (arm,rung,seed) cells produced BYTE-IDENTICAL stdout, sha256 a45b68123b912d227ebcfd2e4365c16765966a04bcfe58d2e2150a7700885141 for both (gates: certificate/twin/headroom/separation/host-validity true, engagement_valid FALSE, repeat false; primary_lift_lb95 -0.1958..., kill_b_fired true).
pin_resolution_carryover: registry/corrections/rules-1-c/1-prefinal-harness-manifest-pin.json (seq 1) resolved the pins.harness_manifest PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder to the MEASURED staged-bytes manifest sha 2b463dd8b0039a8e22e0f10aea6db2b942ddae163a4eae38319f2c1669510bd3 against the superseded freeze 09b246dc. The placeholder text is byte-identical in the refrozen record and the staged bytes it names are unchanged (poc/rules-1/results-incoming/20260712-142704-rules1b-parallel/staged-manifest-sha.txt records the same sha for the merged campaign; per-slice provenance-modal.json local_manifest_matched=true), so the seq-1 resolved value CARRIES OVER verbatim to frozen_sha256 685e3cca.
why_not_correction_only: A posthoc-erratum correction record (a5-llm seq-1 precedent) leaves the defective pin frozen and merely documents it — verdict-gen would STILL be unable to execute the pinned script, so registry/verdicts/rules-1-c.json could never be produced by the registered grader and RULES-2's sequencing gate would stay closed (or need a hand-authored verdict outside the pure-function path, which G-4 forbids). An ops amendment cannot fix it either: /pins/analysis_script is design scope (verdict-gen DESIGN_SCOPE_PREFIXES => ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND). The pre-final reset-refreeze is the CLEANEST lawful path and the only one that keeps the verdict a pure function of (frozen record, chained log).
